OF | Zebra Technologies | ## 1. OVERVIEW The purpose of this instruction is to assist leadership in performing a 5S audit and posting 5S audit results. \*Scope/What is 5S and benefits. ## 2. AUDIT ACCESS **1.** Navigate to Kenosha Distribution SharePoint > '5S' > select either the 'Daily 5S Checklist' or the 'Monthly 5S Checklist, depending on your responsibilities. ![](_page_0_Picture_6.jpeg) **2.** Open the audit form, enter your name, shift, and area, then click "Next". ## 3. SCORING CRITERIA The scoring criteria are divided into three categories: - 1) 0 Does not meet expectations; there is no evidence of consistent category management, or the category management is lacking in the area. - 2) 2—Category management meets some expectations, but it is not consistent throughout the area, and opportunities for improvement still exist. - 3) 4—Exceeds expectations; category management is well executed throughout the area, with no gaps or inconsistencies. # 4. SORT - **1.** Ensure all necessary equipment is present in the area. - ✓ Make note that equipment labeled for the work area is present in the correct amount, functional, and appropriate for the job function. - **2.** Ensure all necessary supplies are present in the area. - ✓ Supplies are any consumable items, like dunnage paper, labels, corrugated materials, and printer supplies. *This document contains proprietary information which is the exclusive property of Zebra Technologies Corp.* **The most recent version of this document is located on the Kenosha Sharepoint Digital Training Library. Verify revision number before use.**

OF | Zebra Technologies | | | | - **3.** Excess supplies and equipment are removed from the area. - ✓ Look for excessive equipment, like extra tape guns, or overstock of supply items like additional label rolls; most consumable items have minimum and maximum quantities listed, while equipment items are singular in quantity. - **4.** Remove any items deemed unnecessary, damaged, defective, obsolete or expired. - ✓ Look for items that are not labeled, damaged, dysfunctional, expired, or do not belong in the area; Escalate to the supervisor, and move to the Red Tag area if present. - ✓ The red tagging area is indicated with the red tape on the floor and a floor sign. - ✓ When placing material or equipment in the red tag area, make sure to fill out the Red Tag form that can be found in the supervisor's area. - **5.** Remove any documents, SWIs, job aids, or labels not needed at the workplace. - **6.** Re-order any equipment that cannot be repaired. - **7.** Log any inconsistencies/opportunities for improvement into the comment section. - ✓ It is crucial to provide a detailed description if the score is imperfect; it helps institute a corrective action response. ## 5. SET IN ORDER

- **1.** Check that each equipment and consumable has a designated location.
  - ✓ Take note of any possible label damage and communicate it to the engineers for replacement.
  - ✓ Make sure to allocate and label a designated spot for newly introduced equipment or consumables.
- **2.** Check that the stations, racking units, and mobile workstations are labeled.
  - ✓ Escalate to engineering if any new or replacement labels need to be generated.
  - ✓ If the new workstation/storage unit/mobile station is introduced to the area, label it.
- **3.** Check that any equipment staging, WIP pallet staging, and raw material/finished good staging are outlined and labeled. Ensure the walkways, emergency exits, and keep-out areas are not obstructed.

![](_page_1_Picture_19.jpeg)

![](_page_1_Picture_20.jpeg)

![](_page_1_Picture_21.jpeg)

#### **CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION WORK INSTRUCTIONS Zebra Technologies** 5S LEADERSHIP AUDIT **Work Instruction No. Revision No.** 01 **Site Code** WI03 **Page** 3 OF 8

*\*Pallet Staging Area \*Clear Walkways (LTL) \*Finished Goods Staging*

- ✓ Ensure that the equipment, WIP, and raw material/finished goods are staged in designated staging areas.
- **4.** Ensure that personal items are not mixed with equipment, consumables, or products in the workplace.
  - ✓ Personal items should be stored out of sight and not mixed with job-related items.

![](_page_2_Picture_7.jpeg)

- **5.** Log any inconsistencies/opportunities for improvement into the comment section.
  - ✓ If the score is imperfect, it is crucial to provide a detailed description; this helps institute a corrective action response.

## 6. SHINE

- **1.** Ensure the work surfaces are clean from trash, dust, unauthorized label residue, written markings and debris. - ✓ When removing the labels, use a heat gun on medium to high settings to heat the label adhesive, pry the corner of the label, and slowly remove it while heating it; use Goo-Gone on the remaining label residue. - ✓ Always wear gloves when working with a heating gun and Goo-Gone for safety. - **2.** Ensure the floor surface is clean from dirt, dust, written markings, oil residues, trash, and debris. - ✓ Regularly cleaning the floor is crucial for associate safety and prevents debris from impeding material handling equipment like pallet jacks, packing carts, etc. - **3.** Always check for material handling equipment, raw material, WIP, and finished goods stored within the designated staging area. - ✓ It is crucial to ensure the material is stored in the designated staging areas, easily accessible and the pedestrian walkways are not blocked. \*Personal items in designated receptacle | CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION | | | | | |-----------------------------------------|----------------------|--------------------|--|--| | WORK INSTRUCTIONS | | | | | | 5S
LEADERSHIP

OF | Zebra Technologies | | |

- ✓ Material handling equipment, like carts and pallet jacks, should be stored within designated staging areas when not in use for ease of access and to prevent employees from wasting time searching for equipment. - **4.** Ensure that only necessary cleaning supplies and equipment are stored in the area, labeled and properly stored. - ✓ Check to ensure that the cleaning solutions used in the area are entered into the CHEMWATCH form and have all applicable safety data sheets. - ✓ If you need assistance entering items in CHEMWATCH, refer to an EHS professional on site for assistance. - **5.** Ensure that trash and dunnage are routinely collected and removed from the area at the end/beginning of each shift. - ✓ Make sure the trash and recyclable materials are segregated and disposed of in the proper bin. - ✓ Separate corrugated packaging material from the rest of the recyclable items and dispose of it in the designated Gaylords for corrugated material. ![](_page_3_Picture_8.jpeg) ![](_page_3_Picture_9.jpeg) ![](_page_3_Picture_10.jpeg) *Blue – Trash Grey – Recyclables Gaylord – Corrugate* - **6.** Log any inconsistencies/opportunities for improvement into the comment section. - ✓ It is crucial to provide a detailed description if the score is imperfect; it helps institute a corrective action response. ## 7. STANDARDIZE **1.** Check that the training materials, job aids, forms or documents about the work being performed have a revision posted, and only the latest revision is available to associates. | CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION | | | | | | |-----------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------|--|--|--|--| | WORK INSTRUCTIONS | | | | | | | 5S

Metadata: "Sub heading": "7. STANDARDIZE: ........**1.** Check that the training materials, job aids, forms or documents about the work being performed have a revision posted, and only the latest revision is available to associates. | CONFIDENTIAL IN............"

Text:
- ✓ Checking training materials and forms in the area helps ensure that the employees perform to the latest standard established within the operation.
- ✓ If some materials and forms are not revised, they must be escalated to the training specialist for correction.
- **2.** Make sure that the production areas have uniform standardized labels. Replace any damaged or worn-out labels by escalating to engineering to print the new ones.

*Replace any damaged floor signs.*

![](_page_4_Picture_5.jpeg)

- **3.** Make sure that the material and equipment are identified with standard floor signs and floor tape
  - ✓ Escalate to engineering for any damaged floor signs or tape that need replacement.
- **4.** Check that the workstation and area design conform to ergonomic standards.
  - ✓ Workstation height for a standing operator should be between 28" and 43".
  - ✓ Workstation height for a standing operator should be between 31" and 38".
  - ✓ The top of the monitor (if present) should be at least eye level.
  - ✓ Ensure the workplace is free from tripping hazards and exposed sharp corners.
  - ✓ Validate that the pedestrian walkways and keep-out areas are not obstructed.
  - ✓ Confirm that the workstation does not have cables dropped on the floor, exposed wiring, or multiple power strips connected in sequence.
  - ✓ Ensure that necessary PPE (if any) and material handling equipment are present in the area and are easily accessible by the operators.
- **5.** Ensure no food or drinks are in unauthorized containers.
  - ✓ Unauthorized containers include, but are not limited to, open aluminum cans, containers with an open lid, glass bottles, and straw cups.

![](_page_4_Picture_20.jpeg)

- **6.** Log any inconsistencies/opportunities for improvement into the comment section.
  - ✓ If the score is imperfect, it is crucial to provide a detailed description; this helps institute a corrective action response.

![](_page_4_Picture_23.jpeg)

OF | Zebra Technologies | - **1.** Assess if the 5S audits are continuously conducted by the leadership in your area across both shifts (if applicable). - **2.** If your score is imperfect, record the gaps in the comment section to help identify a corrective action plan. - **3.** Rate the engagement of associates in your area; provide coaching on the importance of 5S, and support the high achievers by recognition. - **4.** Ensure that the results of the monthly 5S audits are regularly posted for associates to see. - ✓ If the poster is damaged, defective, or needs to be remade, contact the engineering team for support. - **5.** Assess how often 5S is mentioned in the morning standup meetings. - ✓ 5S is a cultural change that needs to be continuously reinforced and encouraged at all levels of the organization. - **6.** Log any opportunities for improvement into the comment section for corrective action plan creation. ## 9. UPDATING 5S POSTERS - **1.** 5S Posters have 5 sections that need to be filled out: Dashboard Chart Results, Map Zone Layout, Checklist Results, Audit Findings/5S+1 Non-Confirming Report/Corrective Action Plan, and 5S Team Recognition/Honorable Mentions. - ✓ Layout updates will only need to be posted if the area design changes, e.g., workstation or work area redesign, addition or removal of workstations. - ✓ The remaining sections of the report should be updated after each monthly audit. *\*5S Posters are posted in each Department* **2.** To update the information on 5S posters with the most recent audit results, access the 5S Leadership Audit Report by navigating to the Kenosha Distribution SharePoint > '5S'. ## **CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION WORK INSTRUCTIONS Zebra Technologies** 5S LEADERSHIP AUDIT **Work Instruction No. Revision No.** 01 **Site Code** WI03 **Page** 7 OF 8

- **3.** The main Audit Report page will be shown on the screen. - **4.** Navigate to the "Filter" and select your respective department. ![](_page_6_Figure_3.jpeg) - **5.** Using the Snipping tool, crop the Rubric out of the '5S' page and print. - **6.** The printing menu interface will appear. Select "Landscape" page orientation, then click "Print". - ✓ The main report page should be posted in the "Dashboard Chart Results". - ✓ Use transparent file folders to place the printed pages. - **7.** From the same filtered main report page, click on the most recent record in the table and then hit the yellow button at the top. ![](_page_6_Figure_9.jpeg) - **8.** The detailed checklist score rubric will appear. Hover over the checklist and click on the "Focus Mode" button. - **9.** Adjust the screen to fit the entire rubric, then using the snipping tool, crop out the table. - **10.** Print the rubric similarly to the 'Main Page Report'. - ✓ The rubric should be posted in the "Checklist Results" section. ![](_page_6_Picture_14.jpeg) | CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION | | | | | |---------------------------|-------------------|----------------------|--------------------|--| | WORK INSTRUCTIONS | | | | | | 5S

- **13.** Print the Excel file '5S Action Item Tracker'
  - ✓ A detailed checklist rubric should be posted in the "Checklist Results, Audit Findings/5S+1 Non-Confirming Report/Corrective Action Plan" section.
- **14.** Post the names of associates and leads who have excelled at 5S sustainability and maintenance to the "5S Team Recognition/Honorable Mentions". The nominated individuals will receive recognition/GEM points from Supervisor.
